text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Two eggs per day increased plasma carotenoids compared to an oatmeal breakfast","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Compared to an Oatmeal Breakfast, Two Eggs/Day Increased Plasma Carotenoids and Choline without Increasing Trimethyl Amine N-Oxide Concentrations","entailment":"The quoted result directly reports that consuming two eggs daily raised plasma carotenoid levels relative to an oatmeal breakfast","population":"participants consuming two eggs per day and those consuming oatmeal breakfast","intervention":"two eggs per day","comparator":"oatmeal breakfast","outcome":"plasma carotenoid levels","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Two eggs per day increased plasma choline compared to an oatmeal Concentrations","entailment":"The same quoted result also indicates that plasma choline levels were higher after the two‑egg intervention versus oatmeal","population":"participants consuming two eggs per day and those consuming oatmeal breakfast","intervention":"two eggs per day","comparator":"oatmeal breakfast","outcome":"plasma choline levels","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Two eggs per day did not increase trimethylamine‑N‑oxide concentrations compared to an oatmeal breakfast","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Compared to an Oatmeal Breakfast, Two Eggs/Day Increased Plasma Carotenoids and Choline without Increasing Trimethyl Amine N-Oxide Concentrations","entailment":"The phrase \"without Increasing Trimethyl Amine N-Oxide Concentrations\" shows that the egg intervention did not raise TMAO relative to the oatmeal control","population":"participants consuming two eggs per day and those consuming oatmeal breakfast","intervention":"two eggs per day","comparator":"oatmeal breakfast","outcome":"trimethylamine‑N‑oxide concentrations","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Egg ingestion increases trimethylamine‑N‑oxide production in humans in a dose‑response manner","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Effect of egg ingestion on trimethylamine-N-oxide production in humans: A randomized, controlled, dose-response study","entailment":"The study title indicates that consuming eggs raises TMAO levels and that the effect varies with dose","population":"humans","intervention":"egg ingestion","comparator":"","outcome":"trimethylamine‑N‑oxide production","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"dose‑response","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Co‑ingestion of whole eggs with glucose protects against postprandial hyperglycaemia‑induced oxidative stress in prediabetic men","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Co-ingestion of whole eggs or egg whites with glucose protects against postprandial hyperglycaemia-induced oxidative stress and dysregulated arginine metabolism in association with improved vascular endothelial function in prediabetic men","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports that adding whole eggs to glucose reduces oxidative stress after a meal in prediabetic men","population":"prediabetic men","intervention":"co‑ingestion of whole eggs with glucose","comparator":"","outcome":"postprandial hyperglycaemia‑induced oxidative stress","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Co‑ingestion of whole eggs with glucose improves vascular endothelial function in prediabetic men","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Co-ingestion of whole eggs or egg whites with glucose ... in association with improved vascular endothelial function in prediabetic men","entailment":"The quoted text links the egg‑glucose co‑ingestion to better vascular endothelial function in the studied population","population":"prediabetic men","intervention":"co‑ingestion of whole eggs with glucose","comparator":"","outcome":"vascular endothelial function","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Insulin sensitivity regulates cholesterol metabolism more than obesity","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Insulin sensitivity regulates cholesterol metabolism to a greater extent than obesity: Lessons from the METSIM Study","entailment":"The title states that insulin sensitivity has a larger effect on cholesterol metabolism than obesity does","population":"participants in the METSIM Study","intervention":"higher insulin sensitivity","comparator":"obesity","outcome":"cholesterol metabolism","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Insulin resistance and obesity affect lipoproteins and modify sensitivity to egg feeding","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Effects of insulin resistance and obesity on lipoproteins and sensitivity to egg feeding","entailment":"The title indicates that both insulin resistance and obesity influence lipoprotein profiles and alter how the body responds to egg consumption","population":"individuals with insulin resistance and
